div1.entryFree:
Vestal, a priestess of Vesta: Ven. 752. Lucr. 883. Ant. III, 12, 31. Per. IV, 5, 7. a chaste woman in general: a fair v. throned by the west, Mids. II, 1, 158 (viz Queen Elizabeth). Ironically: the kitchen v. scorned you, Err. IV, 4, 78.
Adjectively: her v. livery, Rom. II, 2, 8 (i. e. her chastity). in pure and v. modesty, III, 3, 38. a v. livery will I take me to, Per. III, 4, 10.
